title: I Ask Clearly and I Ask Directly
subtitle: An affirmation for making the raise conversation without hedging or pre-emptive softening
excerpt: A short daily affirmation for the practice of asking for a raise, a better rate, or improved terms — without diluting the ask before it lands. For use in the morning and immediately before the conversation.

practice.whenToUse: Use in the morning as a daily grounding statement, and immediately before any conversation about pay, rate, or compensation.

content.text: A daily affirmation for the ask: the request made clearly, without the softening hedge that tends to arrive before the number is said.
I ask clearly. I ask directly. My ask is aligned with what this is worth.

Say it three times in the morning, hand on heart. Say it again before entering any conversation about pay, rates, or compensation. The affirmation is a reminder that the ask is allowed — said plainly, without explanation in advance of a response.
